verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease with no canonical TM domain or GPI anchor; it is constitutively secreted into prostatic fluid and circulates as a soluble protein. However, Shen et al. (2025, Clin Cancer Res) demonstrated by FACS on intact VCaP cells, freshly dissociated patient bone metastasis samples, and confocal IF co-localized with EpCAM that KLK2 protein is present on the cell surface of AR+ prostate cancer cells. Multiple surface-directed clinical programs confirm this: pasritamig (KLK2×CD3 bispecific, Phase 3), 225Ac-KLK2 radioligand therapy, and KLK2-targeted CAR-T cells all engage KLK2 on the tumor cell surface. Surface display is restricted to prostate (cancer) cells with little-to-no expression in other tissues, and the mechanism of surface retention in the cancer state is not fully resolved. This maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface (prostate-cancer-restricted, AR-regulated surface pool) with an element of cell_state_induced (oncogenic AR signaling drives expression and surface association).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Molecular mechanism of surface retention unknown (no TM domain); unclear whether surface KLK2 is tethered to a partner or is a zymogen-associated membrane form. Secreted pool may act as therapeutic sink.
